description:
The nfs-utils package can be removed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf remove nfs-utils
rationale:
<tt>nfs-utils</tt> provides a daemon for the kernel NFS server and related tools. This
package also contains the <tt>showmount</tt> program. <tt>showmount</tt> queries the mount
daemon on a remote host for information about the Network File System (NFS) server on the
remote host. For example, <tt>showmount</tt> can display the clients which are mounted on
that host.
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the nfs-utils package is installed: 
$ rpm -q nfs-utils
fixtext:
To remove the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 package nfs-utils run the following command:

$ sudo dnf remove nfs-utils
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must not have the nfs-utils package installed.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must not have the nfs-utils package installed.
stig.vuldiscussion: "nfs-utils" provides a daemon for the kernel NFS server and related tools. This package also contains the "showmount" program. "showmount" queries the mount daemon on a remote host for information about the Network File System (NFS) server on the remote host. For example, "showmount" can display the clients that are mounted on that host.
stig.checktext:
Verify that the nfs-utils package is not installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed nfs-utils

Error: No matching Packages to list

If the "nfs-utils" package is installed, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Remove the nfs-utils package with the following command:

$ sudo dnf remove nfs-utils

title: Uninstall nfs-utils Package
